jokes.text: Mayor Splett, how does it feel to be the hero of 7-Eleven? Oh, I'm no hero. I'm just a mayor.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about heroic act at 7-Eleven
jokes.punchline: Richard deflects with humble non-sequitur about just being a mayor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard character comedy - his innocent misunderstanding of what makes heroism special

jokes.text: If I need another Washington douche, I'll go to the M Street Rite-Aid.
jokes.punchline: Douche as both insult and literal product you buy at pharmacy
jokes.explanation: Clever dual-meaning joke with perfect economy and specificity of location

jokes.text: That's an hour and 29 minutes too long in that former Indian concentration camp.
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina casual offensiveness, though relies on shock value

jokes.text: Good, 'cause the last one looked like the toilet bowl after William F. Buckley ate a thesaurus.
jokes.punchline: Elaborate metaphor about overly intellectual writing
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual metaphor combining highbrow reference with scatological imagery

jokes.text: Wait. I don't have fat arms. That's just criminally insane-- and regular insane.
jokes.setup: Reading death threat about breaking her fat arms
jokes.punchline: Selina focuses on body image insult rather than death threat
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Selina's vanity overriding survival instinct

jokes.text: Just make up some death threats that are nicer.
jokes.setup: Rejecting real death threats for being too mean
jokes.punchline: Requesting fabricated, more flattering death threats
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd Selina logic - wanting death threats that flatter her

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you conjured her! Yeah, right out of my Stephen Glass-hole.
jokes.setup: Made-up story character turns out to be real person
jokes.punchline: Portmanteau of Stephen Glass (fabricating journalist) and asshole
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining journalist reference with profanity

jokes.text: He's my ex-husband.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about husband Andrew Meyer
jokes.explanation: Simple character beat showing Selina's need to distance herself

jokes.text: I can't hear you, so you're gonna have to repeat that.
jokes.setup: Reporter asking about Andrew making deals
jokes.punchline: Selina pretending not to hear bad news
jokes.explanation: Classic politician evasion tactic played for laughs

jokes.text: Maybe in the House you can.
jokes.setup: Comment about running for office from prison
jokes.punchline: Ben's casual suggestion about House of Representatives
jokes.explanation: Dry political insider joke about low House standards

jokes.text: Your unfavorables are rising past 'accidental ethnic slur' right into 'men's room incident.'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant political comedy - specific scandal taxonomy with perfect escalation

jokes.text: This is the face of clinical depression, ma'am. With the hair of a mental patient.
jokes.punchline: Selina's brutal assessment of her daughter's appearance
jokes.explanation: Cringe-worthy maternal cruelty that defines Selina's character

jokes.text: Oh, my kingdom for a beret.
jokes.punchline: Shakespeare reference applied to fashion emergency
jokes.explanation: Clever high-low reference mixing Shakespeare with superficial concern

jokes.text: if anyone should be depressed, it's me, but I am stable as fuck.
jokes.punchline: Selina claiming stability while clearly unstable
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic moment - claiming stability with obvious instability

jokes.text: I wish you hadn't told me that. And yet I'm also glad that you did. I might be able to use it against you. I wouldn't... unless I have to.
jokes.explanation: Great escalating contradiction showing ethical flexibility

jokes.text: Well, this has been a dry fuck on a sandy beach.
jokes.explanation: Memorable vulgar metaphor with vivid imagery

jokes.text: Any luck with the human Mobius strip?
jokes.punchline: Mathematical reference to lawyer's twisted nature
jokes.explanation: Clever geometric metaphor for duplicitous lawyer

jokes.text: I don't look good in jumpsuits. I mean, I do, but I don't. Ya know.
jokes.punchline: Selina contradicting herself about prison fashion
jokes.explanation: Classic Selina vanity overriding serious concerns

jokes.text: call it the Washington Post like a non-asshole
jokes.explanation: Selina calling someone else an asshole while being one

jokes.text: this is like having two different Senators with their hands up my skirt. Which actually happened to me once during a Supreme Court confirmation.
jokes.punchline: Graphic metaphor with disturbing personal anecdote
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor with uncomfortable personal revelation

jokes.text: I thought it was Kent. I assumed it was you.
jokes.setup: Discussing who the Chinese asset is
jokes.explanation: Shows paranoia and lack of trust in the team

jokes.text: The Joseph Goebbels playbook. Accuse the other side of that which you are guilty. Steal from the best.
jokes.punchline: Casually referencing Nazi propaganda with approval
jokes.explanation: Dark political humor about moral flexibility

jokes.text: Just give it to one of those gay-converting Baptist colleges to fund a statue of a gold-plated Jesus fucking a triceratops.
jokes.setup: Need to spend Chinese money on religious things
jokes.explanation: Hilariously specific and blasphemous imagery that escalates perfectly

jokes.text: Don't say his name.
jokes.explanation: Running gag that treats Jonah as unspeakable evil

jokes.text: No, I told Beth that we could go to Arkansas so she could give me a hand job in a hot spring. It's my birthday.
jokes.explanation: Classic Jonah inappropriate oversharing that makes everyone uncomfortable

jokes.text: Your particular brand of crazy is polling very high here in Florida, especially with melanoma-loving swamp fuckers, storm-ravaged climate deniers, and deadbeat dads... and deadbeat moms.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating list of Florida stereotypes with perfect specificity

jokes.text: Thirty-eight dollars? That's like a shitty gift that you get from your asshole grandmother.
jokes.punchline: Jonah's lack of appreciation for modest amount
jokes.explanation: Shows Jonah's entitlement and poor understanding of fundraising

jokes.text: - Whatever. I'd still do her. - I'm down to clown.
jokes.punchline: Crude sexual interest despite age concerns
jokes.explanation: Crude but character-appropriate; not particularly clever

jokes.text: Holy shit, Bruckheimer, when you get an abortion, you're supposed to leave the mangled fetus at the clinic, not staple it to the skeleton of a gay condor and run it for president.
jokes.explanation: Incredibly creative and disturbing imagery, though somewhat overwrought

jokes.text: What Saudi prince's rape dungeon did you finger-trowel your way out of?
jokes.explanation: Creative but gratuitously dark imagery that relies on shock value

jokes.text: Nope, beat you to it.
jokes.setup: Jonah trying to stop his name being said
jokes.explanation: Continuation of running gag about saying Jonah's name

jokes.text: Youngblood? Oh, that's my favorite Rob Lowe movie.
jokes.setup: Meeting Secret Service agent named Youngblood
jokes.explanation: Minor character beat showing Jonah's shallow cultural references

jokes.text: Rerouting my urethra to behind my balls so that I have to sit to pee like a real girl.
jokes.explanation: Disturbing character detail that relies mainly on shock value

jokes.text: Sit your hawk asses down!
jokes.explanation: Dan's aggressive approach to press management with animal metaphor

jokes.text: Except for the 7-Eleven truthers. They say that no Jewish people were working in the store that day, but there's no Jewish people in Lurlene, so...
jokes.setup: Discussing conspiracy theories about his heroism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - accidentally debunking conspiracy with simple truth

jokes.text: there's no dipping your quill in the company ink. Copy that. I mean sex. Got it. By that, I mean Willa.
jokes.explanation: Good escalating clarification showing Richard's earnestness

jokes.text: Is that a new hairstyle?
jokes.punchline: Softball question instead of hard news
jokes.explanation: Perfect Mike character moment - asking fluff questions

jokes.text: Are they biased against you? Well, you said it, not me, but I also say it.
jokes.explanation: Classic politician non-denial denial

jokes.text: Yeah, I didn't get Clay vaccinated because it causes autism, and now he just has a little bit of autism.
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about anti-vaccine ignorance, but relies on disability jokes

jokes.text: How would anyone know?
jokes.setup: Jonah saying he'll digress from prepared remarks
jokes.punchline: Questioning if he had prepared remarks
jokes.explanation: Good meta joke about Jonah's incoherent speaking style

jokes.text: When are you from? When! Are! You! From?!
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation turning age question into time-travel inquiry

jokes.text: What, are they giving away free Tommy Bahama dick cozies?
jokes.punchline: Specific brand reference with crude imagery
jokes.explanation: Great brand-specific humor with perfect Florida audience targeting

jokes.text: You mean your wisdom stripes.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gary moment - turning negative into positive with creative language

jokes.text: While we're at it, why don't we show 'em my estrogen patch prescription and a Polaroid of my stretch marks?
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd personal disclosure

jokes.text: I believe it's 53. Late 40s. You're all correct.
jokes.setup: Different people giving different ages for Selina
jokes.explanation: Good escalating contradiction about age lying

jokes.text: Me so complicit. Me go jail long time.
jokes.setup: Gary realizing his involvement in scandals
jokes.punchline: Broken English reference to Full Metal Jacket
jokes.explanation: Movie reference adapted to current situation with accent humor

jokes.text: we're gonna keep our wangs out of your Chinese fuckin' finger trap.
jokes.explanation: Clever metaphor mixing children's toy with political entanglement

jokes.text: he's always had a thing for Asians. So do I
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable racial humor that reveals character but relies on inappropriate content

jokes.text: This was supposed to be just a little simple bit of election-rigging!
jokes.punchline: Selina's casual description of major crime
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - minimizing massive corruption as minor inconvenience

jokes.text: You wear it. Maybe you'll grow some hair on your vagina!
jokes.explanation: Crude insult that's more shock than craft

jokes.text: That's good enough for me. Can't argue with science.
jokes.setup: Hearing about seven studies paid for by company
jokes.punchline: Richard's innocent acceptance of obvious bias
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard naivety about corporate manipulation

jokes.text: Oh! Amy. Yeah, Amy. Reminds me, I gotta Apple Pay her for my half.
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about modern payment methods for sensitive situations

jokes.text: When women fall apart, they really fuckin' fall apart!
jokes.explanation: Character-appropriate but lazy gender stereotyping

jokes.text: you need to use tweezers this time because I really thought I could taste your fingers.
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable intimacy showing weird Gary-Selina dynamic

jokes.text: She had done a gap year abroad. She was not a true freshman.
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andrew's sleazy rationalization

jokes.text: I should have really married that gay guy my mother liked.
jokes.explanation: Good character regret with family dynamics subtext

jokes.text: I know him as Luther. We met in Macao at the shark fights.
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurd detail that sounds exactly like criminal underworld

jokes.text: I don't think people are gonna buy that a guy who calls vaginas 'crinkum-crankums' is gonna be able to pull off some sort of multimillion-dollar fraud.
jokes.punchline: Gary's innocent vocabulary vs. sophistication needed
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character analysis with perfect made-up Gary vocabulary

jokes.text: Besides: women's prison. Yeah. Wall-to-wall crinkum-crankum.
jokes.punchline: Casual discussion of prison sexual assault with Gary's vocabulary
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that goes too far into prison rape territory

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. No, that makes sense, I was born in Toronto.
jokes.setup: Can't find birth certificate in New Hampshire
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation he was born in Canada
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - birth certificate issue becomes citizenship issue

jokes.text: ♪ Puss-ay-ay-yay ♪ ♪ In that puss-ay-ay-yay ♪
jokes.explanation: Pure cringe character moment with minimal craft

jokes.text: I think I can see you nodding your head 'yes'...
jokes.setup: Trying to get Marjorie to admit guilt on recording
jokes.punchline: Selina narrating what she wants to hear
jokes.explanation: Good visual comedy about creating false evidence

jokes.text: by some magical combination of abuse and neglect managed to create the perfect daughter.
jokes.setup: Talking about Selina and Andrew as parents
jokes.explanation: Brilliant observation about how terrible parents accidentally succeeded

jokes.text: Why would you buy that cow? Oh. When I can get the milk for free.
jokes.punchline: Selina's inappropriate metaphor for her daughter
jokes.explanation: Classic inappropriate Selina moment ruining a sweet moment

jokes.text: I told you I reported it sunk to insurance.
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Andrew's casual criminality

jokes.text: You can't just disappear, and I can't have people thinking I'm Jewish. We're right in the middle of the primaries.
jokes.setup: Andrew wanting to flee to Israel
jokes.punchline: Selina's political concerns about perceived religion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina priorities - political image over husband's safety

jokes.text: Basically a two-woman race to see who is less offensive to the American people. That's the best explanation of democracy I've ever heard.
jokes.punchline: Cynical but accurate assessment of democracy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant dark political commentary disguised as election analysis

jokes.text: You spent millions... Yes, yes, I did. ...on leather-bound hobo toilet paper.
jokes.setup: Gary spent money on Bibles for homeless
jokes.punchline: Selina's crude reframing of religious gesture
jokes.explanation: Great escalation showing different perspectives on charity

jokes.text: I was in a relationship with a blonde, uncaring bug-eyed rage-aholic. I used to work with someone just like that.
jokes.punchline: Obvious reference to Selina without naming her
jokes.explanation: Good meta moment where Mike describes Selina without naming her

jokes.text: Are you gonna keep that? Morning! Shh!
jokes.explanation: Minor timing joke about interruption

jokes.text: My daughter Catherine is getting married to her lady friend Marjorie.
jokes.setup: Special announcement to distract from scandals
jokes.punchline: Awkward phrasing of daughter's lesbian engagement
jokes.explanation: Characteristic awkward phrasing of progressive announcement

jokes.text: Hilarious. A talking company. Where would the mouth even be?
jokes.explanation: Perfect Richard moment - innocent literal thinking destroying serious moment

jokes.text: A... lledgedly.
jokes.setup: Selina learning Andrew died going to Cuba
jokes.punchline: Ben's careful legal language about destination
jokes.explanation: Perfect timing and legal caution in tragic moment

jokes.text: That's not Rosa. N-- Oh? Oh! There she is. That must be your mother. My sister. Your si-- sister. OK.
jokes.punchline: Selina's confused fumbling with family relationships
jokes.explanation: Awkward photo-op moment showing Selina's discomfort with real people

jokes.text: President Montez. Sure, why not.
jokes.setup: Rosa mistaking Selina for wrong person
jokes.explanation: Good character moment of defeat and mistaken identity

jokes.text: I distinctly remember you saying 'Take care of it.' OK, sure, but I didn't-- I didn't say it in a 'take care of it' kind of way. I just said take care of it.
jokes.setup: Keith claiming he was told to kill Andrew
jokes.explanation: Great escalating confusion about euphemism interpretation

jokes.text: Because I have had Botox!
jokes.setup: Keith saying her eyebrows didn't move
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina moment - vanity procedures causing murder confusion

jokes.text: Like a brain-damaged Chinaman. Or woman. Or woman. Sorry. Or the adopted child of someone with brain damage.
jokes.setup: Needing Mandarin translator who won't understand implications
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortable escalation that relies too much on offensive stereotypes

jokes.text: Name: Jonah Joy Ryan. Weight: six pounds. Length: twenty-six inches. Yeah, that's right, twenty-six inches.
jokes.punchline: Jonah's proud emphasis on unusual baby length
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jonah's weird pride in being freakishly long baby

jokes.text: Lloyd is my real dad? Of course he is. I tried to tell you a hundred times, Jonie.
jokes.explanation: Good family dysfunction escalation with casual revelation

jokes.text: We didn't tell anyone because Lloyd and I are second cousins. God, Mom, that is vomitrocious.
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with perfect Jonah-esque made-up word

jokes.text: Wait, is Lloyd still my dad? Yeah, nobody knows. But you're now officially my stepsister. Half sister.
jokes.explanation: Great escalating family dysfunction with relationship corrections

jokes.text: Is this what it feels like to be a man?
jokes.explanation: Sharp observation about gender and consequence-avoidance

jokes.text: Goddamnit! We can't even commit treason right?!
jokes.setup: Learning Chinese plan was to help Montez
jokes.explanation: Perfect Selina frustration - even her treason is incompetent

jokes.text: I think it's pronounced half-white.
jokes.explanation: Edgy wordplay that relies on racial content

jokes.text: Ha! That was your phone.
jokes.setup: Dramatic moment about leaving no choice
jokes.punchline: Deflation that it was just phone notification
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection deflating dramatic moment
